alternative_branches.fermion_determinant: r filled same-orientation eigenlines induce k=sum C_a; r=2 can give |k|=2, but flavor count, filling, and signs must be independently fixed
alternative_branches.mixed_WZW: a quantized mixed WZW coefficient can reduce to k=n_c B and is nonrenormalization-protected, but an n_c=2 UV completion with the correct global coset is not present in this repository
alternative_branches.tangent_Dirac: T_CP1=O(2) gives a three-section Spin-c/Dolbeault index only after the physical mode is proved tangent-valued; this is deferred to AP-E4

checks.name: all AP-E3 input sources exist and are hashed
AP-E2 exact regression is green but explicitly leaves the Berry level underived
the bare Bose-Hubbard window 0<mu<U locks exactly one constituent on each orbital
the bare analytic charge gap min(mu,U-mu) matches the enumerated spectrum

checks.detail: mu<0 ground n=0; mu>U ground n=2; counterexample E11=-2.955000, E22=-5.860000
checks.name: bare-window limits and the interacting false-inference counterexample are detected
a proved sufficient inequality selects the unique interacting (1,1) plateau
the ferromagnetic Hund Hamiltonian is Hermitian and globally SU(2)-invariant
exact diagonalization gives a triply degenerate triplet and one singlet
the three low states have S^2=2 and the high state has S^2=0
the spin-one projector equals the symmetric-exchange projector
the singlet exclusion gap is exactly Delta_singlet=J_H
reversing the Hund sign selects a singlet and destroys the |k|=2 triplet

checks.detail: 64 directions; max spectral residual=8.88e-16; min gap=0.200000000000
checks.name: a weak orientation field gives a unique gapped ground-state line over every n in S2

checks.detail: samples=256; max norm/map residual=9.99e-16; max singlet leakage=0.00e+00
checks.name: the symmetric tensor product is the normalized quadratic Veronese state
the pair transforms with phase weight two
the pair connection doubles while the declared microscopic kinetic action has signed k=-2
the Veronese pullback quantum metric is twice the single-doublet metric
the ket transition has winding -2 and its dual prequantum line has Chern number +2
direct curvature quadrature converges to dual-prequantum first Chern number two

checks.detail: dimension=3; max Casimir residual=4.44e-16; commutator residual=2.22e-16
checks.name: the locked pair is the three-state spin-one representation

checks.detail: levels 1,2,3 all invariant; max phase residual=2.20e-15
checks.name: large-gauge invariance quantizes k but does not uniquely select signed k=+2
opposite Berry orientations cancel to k=0 rather than |k|=2

checks.detail: single Hilbert dimension=2 at |k|=1; pair dimension=3 at |k|=2; singleton_exclusion_derived=False
checks.name: a permitted single constituent would retain an unwanted |k|=1 doublet

checks.detail: unlocked product Hilbert dimension=4; symmetric locked subspace dimension=3
checks.name: without Hund locking the target is CP1xCP1 rather than its diagonal CP1
an explicit benchmark satisfies portal, temperature, and drive scales below every retained gap

checks.detail: bad portal=0.220000; min gap=0.200000; hierarchy pass=False
checks.name: a portal above the smallest gap is rejected by the scale-separation gate

checks.detail: magnitude-two proof complete; aligned action k=-2; signed orientation, UV field content, and Route-E identification remain open
checks.name: the declared dimer derives |k|=2 while the signed Route-E orientation and UV selection remain open

derived_chain: for H_portal=0, C=mu+h/2+J_H/4 < U-J_H/8 is a sufficient global interacting (1,1)-plateau condition
J_H>0 projects the dimer to the symmetric spin-one triplet
the diagonal coherent state is the quadratic Veronese embedding
Berry connections add, so the ket eigenline is O(-2) and its dual prequantum line is O(2)
the aligned displayed action has k=-2; orientation reversal gives k=+2
the dual quantized carrier is H0(CP1,O(2)) with dimension three
remaining_blockers: derive why the UV field content contains exactly two orbitals/constituents
prove odd or single-constituent sectors are absent rather than merely heavy by assumption
embed the dimer and its Mott/Hund interactions into an anomaly-consistent four-dimensional field theory
derive the portal to Route-E families and keep it below charge, singlet, and orientation gaps
show the spin-one triplet is chiral family data rather than an internal vector multiplet
derive the physical orientation/chirality that selects the required signed Route-E level rather than only |k|=2
